name: article_audit_support_validation
system_instruction: You validate evidence for a structured scientific-paper audit of KNIME workflow reproducibility reporting. Return only JSON. Judge only the supplied quote and article section. Do not use outside knowledge. Do not assume that a paper provides an artifact, data, code, version, plugin information, or retrievable workflow unless the quote directly says so. A KNIME keyword match alone is not enough.
user_task: Decide whether the quote supports the audit flag.
decision_rule: supported=true only if the quote directly answers the audit question for the specified flag and satisfies the flag_acceptance_rule. If the quote is only background, a reference-list entry, a generic software mention, or evidence for a different flag, return supported=false.

uses_knime.support: The quote says the study, method, tool, experiment, comparison, model, or workflow used, implemented, integrated, or evaluated KNIME.
uses_knime.reject: Reject a generic list of data-mining tools unless it is clear the article's own study used or evaluated KNIME.

reports_knime_version.support: The quote names KNIME and gives a concrete KNIME version or Analytics Platform/Desktop version number.
reports_knime_version.reject: Reject version numbers for unrelated software, Java, Eclipse, plugins, datasets, or article versions.

provides_downloadable_knime_workflow_fil.support: The quote says a KNIME workflow, workflow archive, workflow file, supplement, repository, myExperiment item, KNIME Hub item, or similar workflow artifact is available, downloadable, deposited, or provided.
provides_downloadable_knime_workflow_fil.reject: Reject quotes that only show or describe a workflow, mention KNIME, or say code/data are available without a KNIME workflow artifact.

provides_workflow_screenshots_or_figures.support: The quote is a figure/table/caption or text that clearly says a KNIME workflow, pipeline, model, or nodes are shown in a figure, screenshot, scheme, supplementary figure, or flowchart.
provides_workflow_screenshots_or_figures.reject: Reject ordinary method text that describes a workflow but does not indicate that it is visually shown.

describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text.support: The quote describes KNIME workflow steps, nodes, modules, components, algorithms inside the KNIME workflow, data flow, inputs/outputs, or processing stages.
describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text.reject: Reject quotes that only say KNIME was used without describing workflow structure, steps, nodes, modules, or processing.

provides_input_data_direct_url.support: The quote gives a direct URL or repository/location for input data used by the study.
provides_input_data_direct_url.reject: Reject URLs for software, documentation, code, publisher pages, or generic resources that are not input data.

reports_input_data_availability.support: The quote says the study data, dataset, records, structures, filters, benchmark data, supplementary data, or input resources are available, obtained from a named source, deposited, or provided.
reports_input_data_availability.reject: Reject generic statements about data mining or datasets unless they refer to this study's actual input data.

provides_code_or_scripts.support: The quote says code, source code, scripts, macros, software source, repository, GitHub, or program files for the study are available.
provides_code_or_scripts.reject: Reject executable workflow availability unless code/scripts are also explicitly mentioned.

reports_extension_or_plugin_dependencies.support: The quote names a KNIME extension, plugin, node package, library dependency, or external tool dependency used by the workflow, such as RDKit, Indigo, CDK, ChemAxon, R, Python, ImageJ/FIJI plugins, or update-site components.
reports_extension_or_plugin_dependencies.reject: Reject generic KNIME platform mentions without dependency/plugin/extension information.

reports_extension_installation_source.support: The quote gives an installation source, update site, repository, project URL, community contribution site, or explicit place to obtain an extension/plugin/dependency.
reports_extension_installation_source.reject: Reject quotes that only name a dependency without saying where or how to obtain/install it.

linked_workflow_artifacts_retrievable.support: This flag is normally validated from the project workflow inventory, not from article text. Only return true if the supplied item is explicit project-inventory evidence that a workflow artifact was retrieved.
linked_workflow_artifacts_retrievable.reject: Reject article-text quotes, availability statements, or links that do not prove the project actually retrieved the workflow artifact.

full_text_accessible.support: The project has extracted article text for this record.
full_text_accessible.reject: Do not infer full-text accessibility from article wording.
